# SDTM LB Domain Specification Document

## 1. Introduction
This document specifies the structure and content of the SDTM LB (Laboratory Test Results) domain, containing 54,828 records. The LB domain contains data from laboratory tests performed on subjects during the course of a clinical trial.

## 2. LBTESTCD Mapping
The LBTESTCD variable represents the short name of the laboratory test or examination. The following mapping conventions are applied:

### 2.1 Test Code Assignment
- Each unique laboratory test is assigned a standard 8-character test code (LBTESTCD)
- Test codes are derived from the CDISC Laboratory Test Code (NLM) controlled terminology
- Non-standard tests are mapped to the closest equivalent standard test code

### 2.2 Test Name Mapping (LBTEST)
- LBTEST contains the full name of the laboratory test
- LBTEST and LBTESTCD have a one-to-one relationship
- Example mappings:
  - LBTESTCD: "ALB", LBTEST: "Albumin"
  - LBTESTCD: "ALT", LBTEST: "Alanine Aminotransferase"
  - LBTESTCD: "BUN", LBTEST: "Blood Urea Nitrogen"
  - LBTESTCD: "CHOL", LBTEST: "Cholesterol"
  - LBTESTCD: "CREAT", LBTEST: "Creatinine"
  - LBTESTCD: "GLU", LBTEST: "Glucose"
  - LBTESTCD: "HCT", LBTEST: "Hematocrit"
  - LBTESTCD: "HGB", LBTEST: "Hemoglobin"
  - LBTESTCD: "PLAT", LBTEST: "Platelet Count"
  - LBTESTCD: "POTASS", LBTEST: "Potassium"
  - LBTESTCD: "SODIUM", LBTEST: "Sodium"
  - LBTESTCD: "WBC", LBTEST: "White Blood Cell Count"

## 3. Unit Conversion Logic
Unit conversions are applied to standardize laboratory results to consistent units across the study.

### 3.1 Standard Units
- Each LBTESTCD is assigned a standard unit of measurement
- Standard units are defined in the CDISC controlled terminology

### 3.2 Conversion Rules
- When original results are in different units, conversion is performed using standard conversion factors
- Conversion factors are documented in the study metadata
- Example conversions:
  - Glucose: mg/dL to mmol/L (divide by 18)
  - Cholesterol: mg/dL to mmol/L (divide by 38.67)
  - Hemoglobin: g/dL to g/L (multiply by 10)
  - Temperature: °F to °C (subtract 32, multiply by 5/9)

### 3.3 Unit Variables
- LBORRESU: Original units as reported by the laboratory
- LBSTRESU: Standardized units after conversion
- LBORRES: Original result value in original units
- LBSTRESN: Numeric result in standard units
- LBSTRESC: Character result in standard units

## 4. Reference Range Handling
Reference ranges are used to determine if laboratory results are within normal limits.

### 4.1 Reference Range Sources
- Reference ranges may be derived from:
  - Laboratory-specific ranges
  - Study-specific ranges
  - Demographic-specific ranges (age, sex, race)
  - Standard literature ranges

### 4.2 Reference Range Variables
- LBNRIND: Normal range indicator (Low, Normal, High)
- LBREFID: Identifier for the reference range used
- LBLORREF: Lower limit of reference range
- LBHIREF: Upper limit of reference range
- LBSTNRLO: Standard lower limit of normal range
- LBSTNRHI: Standard upper limit of normal range

### 4.3 Reference Range Determination
- Reference ranges are determined based on:
  - Subject demographics (age, sex)
  - Specimen type
  - Laboratory processing methods
  - Time of specimen collection (if relevant)

### 4.4 Normal Range Indicator Logic
- LBNRIND is calculated as:
  - "L" if LBSTRESN < LBSTNRLO
  - "N" if LBSTRESN >= LBSTNRLO and LBSTRESN <= LBSTNRHI
  - "H" if LBSTRESN > LBSTNRHI
  - "Missing" if reference range or result is missing

## 5. Specimen Type
Specimen type information is captured to identify the biological material tested.

### 5.1 Specimen Type Coding
- LBSPEC: Specimen type (e.g., "BLOOD", "URINE", "SERUM", "PLASMA")
- LBSPECSB: Specimen subtype (e.g., "VENOUS", "ARTERIAL", "SPOT", "24-HOUR")
- Specimen types are coded using CDISC controlled terminology

### 5.2 Common Specimen Types
- Blood: Whole blood, serum, plasma
- Urine: Random, 24-hour collection, spot
- Other: Cerebrospinal fluid, synovial fluid, saliva

### 5.3 Specimen Collection Details
- LBSPECCNT: Container type
- LBSPECPH: Preservative or additive
- LBSPECCOND: Collection conditions (e.g., "FASTING", "NON-FASTING")

## 6. Supplemental Qualifier Mappings
Supplemental qualifiers provide additional context for laboratory results.

### 6.1 Timing Variables
- LBDTC: Date/time of specimen collection
- LBDY: Study day of specimen collection
- LBENDY: End of specimen collection relative to reference period
- LBELTM: Elapsed time from reference point

### 6.2 Method and Instrumentation
- LBMETHOD: Laboratory method used
- LBMETHODCD: Coded method
- LBINST: Instrument used for analysis
- LBLAB: Laboratory performing the test

### 6.3 Result Qualifiers
- LBSTAT: Result status (e.g., "NOT DONE", "CANCELED")
- LBREASND: Reason not done
- LBTOXGR: Toxicity grade
- LBCAT: Laboratory category (e.g., "CHEMISTRY", "HEMATOLOGY", "URINALYSIS")

### 6.4 Relationship to Other Domains
- LBSEQ: Sequence number
- LBGRPID: Group identifier for related tests
- LBSPID: Sponsor-defined identifier
- LBLNKID: Link identifier to relate records across domains

## 7. Domain Structure
The LB domain contains the following required and conditional variables:

### 7.1 Identifiers
- STUDYID, DOMAIN, USUBJID, LBSEQ, LBGRPID, LBSPID, LBLNKID

### 7.2 Timing
- LBDTC, LBDY, LBENDY, LBELTM

### 7.3 Qualifiers
- LBTESTCD, LBTEST, LBCAT, LBORRES, LBORRESU, LBSTRESN, LBSTRESU, LBSTRESC, LBNRIND, LBLORREF, LBHIREF, LBSTNRLO, LBSTNRHI, LBTOXGR, LBSPEC, LBSPECSB

### 7.4 Supplemental Qualifiers
- LBSTAT, LBREASND, LBMETHOD, LBMETHODCD, LBINST, LBLAB, LBSPECCNT, LBSPECPH, LBSPECCOND

## 8. Data Quality Checks
The following quality checks are applied to the LB domain:

- All required variables are present
- LBTESTCD and LBTEST mappings are consistent
- Unit conversions are correctly applied
- Reference ranges are appropriate for subject demographics
- Normal range indicators are correctly calculated
- Specimen types are valid and consistent with test types

## 9. Conclusion
This specification document defines the structure and content of the SDTM LB domain containing 54,828 records. The domain follows CDISC SDTM implementation guidelines for laboratory test results, including standardized test codes, unit conversions, reference range handling, specimen type identification, and supplemental qualifier mappings.
